Clay Bracelet  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: WRIST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 4
SV MAGIC: +5
Effect: Eye of Zomm (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 20
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: MAG
Race: HUM ERU HIE DEF GNM DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Clay Bracelet
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: WRIST
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 4
SV MAGIC: +5
Effect: Eye of Zomm (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 20
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: MAG
Race: HUM ERU HIE DEF GNM DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Monk
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-28 06:18:45
Page Updated:Fri Apr 21st, 2017

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 30
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Clay Bracelet

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Temple of Solusek Ro


Uploaded November 27th, 2008

__DEL__1593966214579
Guru
120 posts
Can this be Triggered by non-Mages to use the Eye ?

It would be interesting, as a Monk I like to see if the Mob I want to pull isn't surrounded by millions of friends.
▲ Page top
RE: Triggerable By Non-Mages ?
QuoteReply
# Mar 17 2004 at 12:47 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1679271392283
Scholar
308 posts
Certain items that cast class specific spells cannot be clicked by other classes (see: the colored Ceramic Band series of player made items).
▲ Page top
RE: Triggerable By Non-Mages ?
QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2004 at 12:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593374519737
View User Forum
165 posts
Quote:
Certain items that cast class specific spells cannot be clicked by other classes


This is untrue... the Amulet of Necropotence casts the spell Illusion: Skeleton which is an enchanter spell... it is inventory clickable by all classes and equippable by all casters.
▲ Page top
RE: Triggerable By Non-Mages ?
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2004 at 8:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591811630399
14 posts
He said "certain items", not all items. Just wanted to point that out. The Amulet of Overpricedence is not amongst those certain items.
▲ Page top
RE: Triggerable By Non-Mages ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2003 at 5:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757195733
3 posts
If you're wanting to use the Eye of Zomm as a monk, I suggest you get a Stalking Probe. They're tinkered items with five charges of Eye of Zomm. And it is very nice for pulling.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 17 2003 at 10:25 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) says magician class only listed...so my read is nope, not on this life.
RE: Triggerable By Non-Mages ?
QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2003 at 11:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776013048
View User Forum
263 posts
Mages are the only class that can equip this.

Star of Eyes is a wiz only item that can be triggered from inventory by any class. There are many items like that, for which you don't necessarily have to be the class listed to use the item.

I don't know if this Clay Bracelet is like that, though someone below says it can't be triggered by others. I'm just pointing out that the listed class isn't what determines who is able to use the effect.



Edited, Wed Jun 18 11:29:07 2003
▲ Page top
RE: Triggerable By Non-Mages ?
QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2003 at 2:53 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1704672973637
Guru
5,311 posts
Quote:
I'm just pointing out that the listed class isn't what determines who is able to use the effect.


I believe all of the Temple of Solusek Ro quest rewards are restricted to the listed class.

If anyone knows otherwise, please post a correction.

# Sep 29 2002 at 2:11 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1633306622282
429 posts
aren't you guys forgetting eq is a game? if its fun its not worthless.
▲ Page top
Worthless no more.
QuoteReply
# Aug 19 2002 at 5:07 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249716241
Sage
207 posts
To the mages of norrath,

I see that no one has talked about this yet, so I'll mention it.

This little thingamabob is now one of the handiest items in the game. Right up there with me jbooties. And I'll tell ya why.

Since may 8th they changed the way eye of zomm works. Before that patch eye of zomm had a few uses (low aggro pulling), but quite a few disadvantages.

1) It mirrored your faction and aggroed any mob that would be KoS to you, which essentially made it bloody useless for anything but pulling.

2) It counted as a pet which meant you couldn't have a pet summoned while you had an eye up, which made it even more bloody useless.

however, being the stubborn susmnabizsnach that I am I got it anyway. And it sat in me inventory...

and sat.. and sat... UNTIL!!!

May 8th rolled around and the patch changed everything. Now the eye no longer counts as a pet.. and it no longer aggros mobs... and this braclet casts instantly from inventory...

Which means that now by a simple right click or by hitting a hotkey, you can now have a manaless, instant scout for about 30 seconds that will not aggro, will not train, and will allow you to get REALLY neat pics of everything from vox to inny that hateful bastich himself... without any drawbacks what so ever. You can scout pulls, peek around corners... look up cute wood elf dresses...

err, ignore that last one... ANYWAY the point is, this thing is now INSANELY useful. This is one of the safest ways to look at mobs that see invis without getting aggro. And ya can't beat a manaless instant cast with a stick.

So yes, this does work from inventory and IMHO every mage in EQ outta have one. I use this sucker constantly.

CLAY BRACELET SUX NO MORE!!!




Faelawen Starfyre, The Mad Mage of Povar

52 1/2th season, leader of the Defiant Heroes, King of the Karanas, Arch nemesis of the Evil Squirrel Empire.

PS: Fnord.

PPS: which raises the next question, what level ya have to be to trigger it. I know it worked for me at 32, but aside from that I am unaware at how soon ya can hope to snag this lil beauty.

PPPS: also, ya can get this pretty low, but bring help. Some of those necro towers are a pain to clear.

Edited, Mon Aug 19 06:00:30 2002

Sure it's worthless?
QuoteReply
# Oct 06 2001 at 3:48 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591752584201
67 posts
I've heard that you can summon an eye for free, then use your focus item to kill it for mana, and repeat. Also, you can summon up an eye and a necro can tap it for hp. Would either of these work? If so I would think it would be nice to have one on hand.....
▲ Page top
RE: Sure it's worthless?
QuoteReply
# Nov 05 2001 at 4:53 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591758476986
Scholar
62 posts
Mendan, you are absolutely correct. If you look under the Holgresh War Beads item description on this site, you'll see MANY necros offering 20K+ for an all/all version of this piece. This is because we CAN drain the Master-less eye! It has roughly 5hp, but this doesn't stop us from draining it for maximum (depending on spell, natch).

I'm afraid I've never played a Mage, I don't know if the same trick can be played by that class.


Markoff Chaney
Gnecro of the 44th Corpse
▲ Page top
RE: Sure it's worthless?
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2001 at 5:25 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
dont these eyes only have 1hp?
▲ Page top
RE: Sure it's worthless?
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2001 at 5:09 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591759157191
View User Forum
Guru
179 posts
Hey.

The amount of health a necro receives from a lifetap is not directly related to the amount of health a victim has to lose.

If I cast Vampiric curse, I gain something in the region of 200HP. (Over a little more than a minute.) Assuming my victim has this many HPs left, they will lose this many HP. However, I will gain the full amount regardless. So, if I cast on a level one monster, I will still gain full benefit.

This is definitely the case with the DoT lifetaps. I am not 100% certain it's true of DD lifetaps, since it never occurred to test it. Having said this, I'd be pretty sure of it.

Hope this helps.

AlAaraaf.
"Death is coming..."
▲ Page top
RE: Sure it's worthless?
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2002 at 3:21 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592255506510
7 posts
it works with the lifetaps too.. its fun to watch the life of a rat go from full to nothing with one cast while my health goes up over a hunder points (lvl 34)

RE: Sure it's worthless?
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2002 at 9:02 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759409730
View User Forum
174 posts
aye necros can tape it for full effect on ANY lifetap spell. MEANING even a lvl 60 necro can cast Zevfeer's Theft of Vitae ( 1050 group life tap over time) on an eye of zomm and everyone will get the full effect of it. as for the rclaim energy, they changed it to where it it no longer counted as a pet wiping that out =\

Edited, Tue Nov 12 08:45:04 2002

Not worth the time
QuoteReply
# Aug 17 2001 at 10:35 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361532627
View User Forum
54 posts
Hate to post stuff like this on here, but fellow Mages, don't waste your time on this one. The other Sol Ro quests are real nice, but stats like this for having to kill these four baddies is truthfully not worth it. When you go to Upper Guk to kill the Tonta Knight for your Earthen boots, go to the little temple room and camp the Froglok Scryer and grab a couple bracelets of woven grass. Good luck mages!
▲ Page top
RE: Not worth the time
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2002 at 9:34 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361638914
View User Forum
Sage
402 posts
But one thing to keep in mind. Some of us have hit the soft cap in Int and don't need one or two +3Int bracers. For me, this was a very easy quest and gave me +5 save vs magic (which is needed later in your career). By all means if you still need more +Int go for the braclets of woven grass but this is a nice alternative until something else (planar, etc) stuff shows up.
▲ Page top
RE: Not worth the time
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2003 at 11:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782793772
Scholar
435 posts
And, eighteen months later, with LDoN this has an augment slot. Mine now has +5 INT as well as a +5 save, and the clicky effect: that makes it a pretty nice bracer compared to many of the other options available.

▲ Page top
RE: Instant?
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2001 at 3:18 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592247845609
Scholar
73 posts
I'm unsure as to whether or not this has been changed, but the eye of Zomm use to be a level 1 mob (correct, mob, not pet) with a bind sight, which any player can attack it. It also happens to have... hmm... limited health? Somewhere in the neighbourhood of... well, 2? Heh ;) Pretty easy for any newby to deal 2 damage in one hit. Considering the eye is completely incapable of retalliation, this would be a cause for a pretty short trip to level 6. If it was an instant cast click, and not a time delayed click, that is. If not, heck, it's still probably easier than finding an unlimited supply of mobs in most zones. =P
▲ Page top
Caeadyen
QuoteReply
# Jan 17 2001 at 5:27 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Just wanted people to know what I suspect with this and the other three quests Joyce the NPC gives out at the Temple of Solesek Ro. She calls them Items of the Elementalist and they are four all together, which are:

1.) Robe of the Elements - I did this quest today at lvl 30 because I wanted the robe since I saw someone wearing it at lvl 8. When I got the robe Joyce said, and I quote, "The Robe of the Elements is a fine cloak for a magician. It provides protection from the elements, protection from melee attacks, and aids the magician with her focus." Nowhere in the rightclick description box for the robe does it say something about the robe helping with focus, but Joyce did.

2.) Clay Bracelet

3.) Earthen Boots

4.) Circlet of Mist


Note that there are 4 elemental style pets (Fire, Water, Earth, and Air), 4 focus items (torch, stein, shovel, and broom), and 4 Items of the Elementalist (Robe of the Elements, Clay bracelet, Earthen Boots, and Circlet of Mist). It seems (I'm stretching here) that the earthen boots are linked to earth and that the Circlet of Mist may be linked to Air (look in the mage research books ingame and you will see that the spell names for summoning air are really called summon mist - that is if you can read dragon). Maybe the Clay bracelet is for water (aren't some Steins to hold water made out of clay? - although in German that word means stone) and the Robe is for Fire or maybe you need all the items together. Who knows.

I am going to do all the mage quests and I'm gonna wear the items and have fun being a mage. Don't take this simple looking item for granted. Who knows, maybe I'll summon a better pet than you, Muuhhhaaaha (or whater).
▲ Page top
RE: Caeadyen
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2001 at 6:41 PM Rating: Decent
Draggun
21 posts
Okay - to break down what Joyce says

"The Robe of the Elements is a fine cloak for a magician.
1) It provides protection from the elements, - +5 Sv Fire +5 Sv Cold +5
2) protection from melee attacks, and - AC 9
3) aids the magician with her focus" - Int +4

Hope that helps answer the question.

Draggun
